title: TC001-Load monthly calendar dashboard and confirm schedule content is visible
description: Verifies the main monthly calendar view loads and shows shift slot labels for days in the month.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[normalize-space(text())="Monthly calendar"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=1A').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=1B').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=2nd Call').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=3rd Call').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Navigate to next month using the right arrow
description: Verifies month navigation forward updates the visible month on the calendar.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Guest' button to open the app in view-only mode so the calendar can be inspected.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Next month' right arrow button to advance the calendar view to the next month.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div/div/header/div[2]/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Assert the month label is visible (header button)
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/main/div/div/header/div[1]/button')
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        
        # Assert the month label is still visible after navigation
        elem_after = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/main/div/div/header/div[1]/button')
        assert await elem_after.is_visible()
        
        # Assert the text "1A" is visible (specific span containing 1A)
        elem_1a = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/main/div/div/div[1]/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div[2]/span[1]')
        assert await elem_1a.is_visible()
        
        # Assert the calendar grid is visible by checking a day cell in the grid
        grid_cell = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/main/div/div/div[1]/div/div/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/span')
        assert await grid_cell.is_visible()
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Monthly calendar').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Generated schedule').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Monthly calendar').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
